title: Nearly Half of US Kids Are Breathing Dirty Air — and It's Getting Worse
summary: Here's some scary news: nearly half of all children in the United States are breathing air that is dangerous for their health. That's according to a new report from the American Lung Association. The report looked at two main types of pollution: smog (a kind of dirty air that looks like a brown haze) and soot (tiny particles from things like cars and factories). Kids are especially at risk because their lungs are still growing, they breathe more air for their size, and they spend a lot of time playing outside. Breathing dirty air can cause asthma (a lung disease that makes it hard to breathe), long-term damage to lungs, and other health problems. The report also found that communities of color are hit hardest. People of color make up 42% of the US population, but they represent 54% of people living in the most polluted areas. Why is the air getting worse? One big reason is climate change — hotter temperatures and more wildfires create more pollution. Another reason is that the government has been rolling back (removing) many rules that protected the air. For example, they've weakened limits on pollution from power plants and cars. "There's a devaluing of children's health by this EPA," said Will Barrett from the American Lung Association. That means the agency that's supposed to protect our air isn't doing enough to keep kids safe. The good news? We can still fight for cleaner air — and our health depends on it.
why_it_matters: This affects you directly — the air you breathe every day could be harming your lungs, and knowing about it is the first step to demanding cleaner air for everyone.

keywords.explanation: Kids in the US whose health is at risk from dirty air.
The air we breathe; nearly half of US children breathe unsafe air.
Dirty substances in the air, like smog and soot, that harm health.
The organization that wrote the report about air pollution.
A lung disease that makes it hard to breathe, worsened by dirty air.
The heating up of Earth, which makes pollution worse by causing more wildfires and heat.

options: People who live in the country

background_read: Air pollution comes from cars, factories, power plants, and wildfires. It can cause health problems like asthma and lung damage.
Children's lungs grow until they are about 18, so dirty air is especially dangerous for them.
The Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) is the US government agency that is supposed to make rules to keep air clean.
Article_Structure: WHO: Nearly half of US children and communities of color
WHAT: Breathing dirty air that can hurt their lungs and cause asthma
WHY: Climate change and the government removing rules that protect air quality

perspectives.description: Parents should be concerned because dirty air is harming children's health and may cause long-term problems.
The government is not doing enough to protect kids; the EPA is weakening rules instead of strengthening them.
We can still fight for cleaner air, and the report shows that the problem is known, so we can work to fix it.
